title: ExecutionReport
description: Structured return value from every execution-style workflow. Consumed by Opus post-run for cross-wave merges, progress YAML updates, plan-completion artifact, and Mode D escalation handling.

status.description: 'complete': all waves finished, all reviewers approved. 'blocked': a Mode D boundary was hit — Opus must run that phase interactively. 'needs_opus': reviewer escalation unresolved, budget exhausted, or a human-assigned (HITL) task gates the next wave — Opus must adjudicate.

reason.description: Present when status is 'blocked' or 'needs_opus'. Indicates the specific cause. 'hitl_required': the wave's agent work + reviewer gates completed but one or more human-assigned tasks must be signed off before advancing (see hitl_tasks). 'scope_exceeds_single_pass' / 'spike_required' / 'plan_only' / 'plan_structure_failed' are autopilot-lane reasons (auto-feature workflow): respectively, the planned work exceeds single-pass capacity and needs full planning; unresolved research unknowns require a SPIKE/exploration first; planning ran with plan_only=true and awaits a go-ahead to execute; or the plan-structuring stage failed and Opus must read the plan artifact on disk.

blocked_phase.description: When status is 'blocked', the id of the phase that triggered the Mode D boundary.

hitl_tasks.description: Present when reason is 'hitl_required'. The pending human-assigned tasks gating progression. Opus coordinates the human review (future: external task-tracker / intent-tree review request), then relaunches with these tasks marked complete / trimmed.

phase.description: Phase id the HITL task belongs to.

assigned_to.description: The human assignee (non-agent), e.g. a username.

prompt.description: What the human is asked to validate / sign off.

report.description: Per-wave results in execution order. Partial — may contain only completed waves if the run was interrupted.

autopilot.description: Present only on returns from the auto-feature (autopilot) workflow. Carries the classification + the durable plan artifact location so Opus can act on escalations or merge on success. Other execution workflows omit this field.

tier.description: Tier the planner classified the request into.

wave_count.description: Sequential dependency depth of the planned ExecutionGraph.

plan_artifact_path.description: Repo-relative path to the durable plan/contract artifact the planner wrote. Survives escalation so full planning gets a head start.

execution_target.description: Which engine the autopilot dispatched to (or 'none' when it escalated before executing).

escalation_recommendation.description: Human-readable next-step recommendation when the feasibility gate escalated.

wave.description: Wave id matching the ExecutionGraph wave.

phase.description: Phase id matching the ExecutionGraph phase.

fix_cycles.description: Number of fix-loop iterations consumed. 0 means reviewer approved on first pass.

escalate.description: True when fix_cycles reached the maximum and verdict.approved is still false. Triggers needs_opus status at wave level.

files_touched.description: Union of files_affected across completed tasks in this phase. Used by Opus to scope worktree merge.

hitl_gates.description: Pending human-assigned (HITL) tasks in this phase. Aggregated at wave level into the top-level hitl_tasks field, which triggers reason:'hitl_required'.

status.description: 'skipped' when agent() returned null (user skipped). 'failed' when the agent threw and was dropped by parallel().

commit_sha.description: Git SHA of the commit authored by this agent in its worktree. Null/absent if no commit was made (e.g. read-only task, or task failed).

summary.description: Brief summary of what the agent did. At most 2 sentences.

reviewer_type.description: agentType of the reviewer that produced this verdict.

required_fixes.description: Present when approved is false. Each item is an actionable fix instruction for the fix-loop agent.

council_artifacts.description: Present only when reviewer_type is 'council-review'. Contains the ARC run artifact paths.

CouncilArtifacts.description: Paths to the ARC run artifacts produced by a council-review gate. All paths are relative to repo root.

run_dir.description: Path to the run directory, e.g. '.claude/skills/council-review/runs/2026-06-01-execute-plan-phase-2'.

findings_yaml.description: Path to findings.yaml within the run directory.

scorecard_json.description: Path to scorecard.json within the run directory.

risk_register_yaml.description: Path to risk_register.yaml within the run directory.

decision_record_md.description: Path to decision_record.md within the run directory.

validation_plan_md.description: Path to validation_plan.md within the run directory.

task_id.description: Task that surfaced the blocker, if applicable.
